status: first-pass evidence bundle exported from tracker rows

observations.observation_value: Five complete chronic traumatic SCI patients underwent SSEP/MEP-guided scar excision, autologous BMMC-loaded NeuroRegen scaffold implantation, and 12-month follow-up.
observations.normalized_value: chronic complete human scaffold implantation feasible in small cohort
observations.statistics_reported: small cohort; adverse-event detail requires full source review
observations.notes: Feasibility signal only; not efficacy proof.

observations.observation_value: Eight chronic complete SCI patients received hUCB-MSC-loaded NeuroRegen scaffold after neurophysiology-guided scar resection and were followed for one year.
observations.normalized_value: chronic complete human NeuroRegen/hUCB-MSC feasibility cohort
observations.statistics_reported: abstract-level; exact outcome statistics not yet extracted
observations.notes: Similar intervention family as M2A-P001 but different cell source.

observations.observation_value: Porous PLGA/poly-l-lysine scaffolds implanted after thoracic lateral hemisection improved locomotor recovery in fully hemisected monkeys compared with non-implanted controls and showed appositional healing/tissue remodeling at 12 weeks.
observations.normalized_value: primate scaffold implantation improved locomotion and remodeling
observations.notes: Strong translational bridge but still abstract-level here.

observations.observation_value: Biomimetic 3D-printed scaffolds loaded with neural progenitor cells supported host axon entry, graft axon extension into host cord, synaptic relay formation, synaptic transmission, and functional improvement after complete SCI.
observations.normalized_value: 3D printed architecture plus NPCs supports bridge/relay repair
observations.notes: Architecture and cell effects are bundled.

observations.observation_value: Porous aligned hydrogel tubes integrated with host tissue, reduced scarring relative to gelfoam, guided axon growth along tube surfaces, supported myelination of some axons, and enhanced functional recovery.
observations.normalized_value: aligned hydrogel tubes support tissue integration and axon guidance
observations.notes: Important scaffold-only guidance anchor.

observations.observation_value: PLGA 85:15 scaffolds were fabricated with parallel channels to enable testing of scaffold architecture, transplanted cells, and local molecular agents for axon regeneration.
observations.normalized_value: multi-channel PLGA scaffold as combinatorial repair platform
observations.notes: Primarily platform/architecture evidence, not direct functional outcome.

observations.observation_value: Self-assembling peptide nanofiber scaffold with neural progenitor and Schwann cells supported host-cell migration, blood-vessel growth, and axonal ingrowth into rat dorsal-column transection sites.
observations.normalized_value: SAPNS creates permissive 3D ingrowth environment
observations.notes: Early peptide scaffold bridge anchor.

observations.observation_value: Aligned CNT/GelMA hydrogel fibers mimicked axon-like conductive aligned structure; combined with electrical stimulation they promoted neural-cell alignment/differentiation in vitro and were tested for SCI recovery in vivo.
observations.normalized_value: conductive aligned scaffold plus ES promotes neural alignment and repair
observations.notes: Advanced guidance/electrical interface anchor.

observations.observation_value: hMSC-seeded PLGA scaffolds implanted into 2-mm complete rat spinal cord defects improved BBB scores and MEP amplitudes relative to scaffold-only controls.
observations.normalized_value: PLGA+hMSC improved function/electrophysiology vs scaffold-only
observations.statistics_reported: abstract reports approximate BBB 5 vs 2 and MEP 200-300 uV
observations.notes: Useful because scaffold-only comparator is present.

observations.observation_value: Schwann cells in injectable decellularized peripheral nerve matrix produced larger implants and about twice as many Schwann-cell-myelinated axons as Matrigel grafts while performing similarly in BBB locomotor testing.
observations.normalized_value: iPN improves graft/axon histology vs Matrigel without clear BBB superiority
observations.notes: Separates histological benefit from locomotor equivalence.

observations.observation_value: Gelatin sponge scaffold with NT-3-overexpressing Schwann cells and TrkC-overexpressing NSCs enhanced myelination and repair after rat T10 complete transection.
observations.normalized_value: engineered cell/scaffold network promotes myelination
observations.notes: Combination mechanism is highly bundled.

observations.observation_value: NT-3 released from fibrin scaffolds increased neuronal fiber growth into adult rat spinal cord lesions by about 1.5- to 3-fold and reduced glial scar formation, but hindlimb motor function did not improve relative to controls.
observations.normalized_value: NT-3 fibrin scaffold improved histology without functional improvement
observations.statistics_reported: 1.5- to 3-fold fiber growth; function not improved
observations.notes: Key positive-histology/negative-function boundary.

observations.observation_value: Structured HA hydrogel with anti-NgR and BDNF/VEGF-loaded PLGA microspheres integrated with host tissue, inhibited inflammation/gliosis, increased vessels and regenerated fibers, and improved locomotor recovery after rat dorsal hemisection.
observations.normalized_value: HA+PLGA multi-cargo scaffold improved repair profile
observations.notes: Multiple active components make attribution difficult.

observations.observation_value: Dual-phase SilMA hydrogel with PLGA microspheres was designed to release Ang-(1-7) early and NT-3 later, reducing inflammation and promoting neural differentiation/axonal regeneration in SCI repair assays.
observations.normalized_value: staged release scaffold targets inflammation then regeneration
observations.notes: Strong design logic; needs full text for timing and effect sizes.

observations.observation_value: Porcine spinal cord and urinary bladder ECM hydrogels integrated into rat hemisection lesions and stimulated neovascularization and axonal ingrowth, while macrophage/mass response created limitations.
observations.normalized_value: injectable ECM supports ingrowth but immune/material response matters
observations.statistics_reported: qPCR/histology reported; exact values not extracted
observations.notes: Important ECM benefit/boundary observation.

observations.observation_value: HA hydrogels containing protoplasmic astrocyte-derived ECM reduced glial scar size and increased axon penetration after SCI compared with other ECM conditions.
observations.normalized_value: astrocyte ECM subtype changes HA hydrogel histology
observations.notes: ECM source/subtype is a material boundary condition.

observations.evidence_type: Chronic scar removal plus anisotropic hydrogel
observations.observation_value: Anisotropic alginate hydrogels implanted after scar removal at 4 WPI in chronic complete thoracic transection supported electrophysiological conductivity, locomotor improvement, host-cell migration, reduced scarring, and multiple tract classes growing through/beyond channels.
observations.normalized_value: chronic scar-removal alginate scaffold supports structural and functional repair
observations.statistics_reported: outcomes up to 3 months; exact stats not extracted
observations.notes: Strong chronic bridge to Module 1, but abstract-level here.

observations.evidence_type: Chronic canine scar removal plus NeuroRegen/hUC-MSC
observations.observation_value: In chronic canine complete T8 transection, delayed scar removal followed by hUC-MSC-loaded NeuroRegen scaffold promoted locomotor recovery, endogenous neurogenesis, and reduced scar response.
observations.normalized_value: large-animal chronic NeuroRegen+hUC-MSC repair signal
observations.notes: Translational large-animal chronic anchor.

observations.observation_value: HA demilune scaffold with PPY-coated fibers, RAD16 hydrogel, iNPCs, and curcumin preserved iNPC viability, increased neurite outgrowth/migration in vitro/organotypic culture, and was applied as a minimally invasive surface cap after subacute SCI.
observations.normalized_value: surface-capping conductive scaffold supports cells and repair-associated responses
observations.notes: Surface-capping strategy differs from lesion-filling bridges.

observations.observation_value: Schwann-cell exosome and methylprednisolone composite patch improved rat SCI functional and electrophysiological performance, shifted macrophages toward M2-type response, reduced inflammatory response, and increased neuronal survival.
observations.normalized_value: exosome/steroid surface patch modulates inflammation and repair
observations.notes: Patch covers hematoma surface rather than filling cavity.

observations.observation_value: OEC-seeded longitudinal collagen scaffold produced functional improvement in acute SCI, but recovery was not correlated with axon regeneration through the scaffold.
observations.normalized_value: functional recovery can dissociate from scaffold-traversing axon regeneration
observations.statistics_reported: abstract-level; exact correlation metrics not extracted
observations.notes: Prevents overinterpreting all recovery as bridge-mediated axon regeneration.

observations.observation_value: Two strictly diagnosed acute complete SCI patients received NeuroRegen scaffold plus MSC transplantation and one-year monitoring.
observations.normalized_value: acute complete human scaffold/MSC feasibility signal
observations.notes: Supports feasibility only because cohort is extremely small.

observations.observation_value: Collagen scaffold plus hUC-MSC strategy was tested in rat/canine complete transection models and a phase I acute complete human SCI trial.
observations.normalized_value: collagen+hUC-MSC tested across animal and human phase I settings
observations.statistics_reported: exact human outcome values not extracted
observations.notes: Important translational bridge but cell/scaffold effects are bundled.

observations.observation_value: Aligned nanofiber-hydrogel scaffold after rat C5 hemi-incision provided nonviral delivery, contact guidance, early aligned axon regeneration, and no excessive inflammatory or scar response.
observations.normalized_value: aligned nanofiber scaffold supports delivery/guidance without excessive scar
observations.notes: Combines scaffold guidance with molecular delivery.

observations.observation_value: Aligned collagen sponge scaffold supported human spinal cord-derived NSPC transplantation after complete transection SCI and was used to compare hSC-NSPCs with brain-derived NSPCs.
observations.normalized_value: aligned collagen scaffold supports human NSPC repair platform
observations.notes: Cell-source comparison is central, but scaffold provides retention/guidance platform.

observations.observation_value: Magnetically responsive self-assembling peptide hydrogels aligned under a 100-Gauss field and were validated for promoting/aliging axon infiltration at SCI sites.
observations.normalized_value: injectable hydrogel topology can be externally aligned
observations.notes: Distinct injectable alignment strategy.

observations.observation_value: GelMA microchannel scaffold loaded with hAEC-derived neural-like cells improved neural circuit and hindlimb recovery after rat full transection SCI.
observations.normalized_value: microchannel GelMA scaffold plus hAEC neural-like cells supports circuit repair
observations.notes: Architecture and cell effects are bundled.

observations.observation_value: Coaxial 3D-printed HA/alginate/gelatin-cellulose scaffold delivered timed cues for oxidative-stress control, endogenous NSC migration, neuronal differentiation, and SCI repair.
observations.normalized_value: printed hydrogel can program time-sequenced repair cues
observations.notes: Also belongs to cargo/release topic.

observations.observation_value: 3D-bioprinted collagen/silk fibroin scaffold with spinal-cord-like cavities plus NSCs promoted nerve regeneration after SCI.
observations.normalized_value: bioprinted silk/collagen scaffold plus NSCs supports regeneration
observations.notes: Printed architecture and cell effect bundled.

observations.evidence_type: Chronic scar resection plus collagen/hUC-MSC scaffold
observations.observation_value: NeuroRegen collagen scaffold plus hUC-MSCs after 12-week chronic rat scar resection was assessed by locomotion, motor/somatosensory evoked potentials, and histological repair.
observations.normalized_value: chronic rat scaffold/cell repair after scar resection
observations.notes: Chronic repair bridge; full text unavailable in tracker.

observations.observation_value: Chronic complete SCI scar resection plus dECM hydrogel and hNS/PC transplantation improved histological repair signals but did not show functional recovery in the Module 2A extraction.
observations.normalized_value: histological repair without functional recovery after dECM+hNS/PC chronic repair
observations.notes: Important chronic histology-function boundary.

observations.evidence_type: Chitosan bridge plus NSPC and immobilized cues
observations.observation_value: Methacrylamide chitosan hydrogel and conduit bridge delivered adult NSPCs with immobilized IFN-gamma or PDGF-AA to bias neuronal or oligodendrocytic lineages after rat hemisection SCI.
observations.normalized_value: chitosan bridge couples cell delivery with lineage cues
observations.notes: A clear example of scaffold/cell/cue confounding by design.

observations.observation_value: dSCM/GelMA composite scaffold provided a bioactive microenvironment for human menstrual blood-derived stem cells and improved SCI functional reconstruction in rat in vivo testing.
observations.normalized_value: spinal cord dECM/GelMA supports stem-cell repair
observations.notes: ECM and cell effects bundled.

observations.observation_value: HA hydrogel with decellularized brain matrix, PDRN, primed MSC EVs, and NPCs induced angiogenesis, anti-inflammatory/anti-apoptotic effects, neural differentiation, regeneration, and functional recovery in rat SCI.
observations.normalized_value: multimodal hydrogel scaffold supports multi-process repair
observations.notes: Highly combinatorial; cannot attribute effect to one component.

observations.observation_value: Gelatin/alginate hydrogel seeded with hEnSCs and curcumin-loaded PLGA nanoparticles improved regeneration and anti-inflammatory repair effects in rat SCI.
observations.normalized_value: hydrogel+cell+drug nanoparticle strategy improves repair profile
observations.notes: Triple combination limits causal attribution.

observations.observation_value: Noggin-loaded nanochitosan/polypyrrole-alginate conductive scaffold supported hAEC attachment/neural differentiation and SCI repair-marker testing in an animal model.
observations.normalized_value: conductive chitosan scaffold supports hAEC differentiation and repair testing
observations.notes: Conductive/cell/growth-factor combination.

observations.observation_value: CBD-modified miR21-loaded exosomes tethered to collagen-I scaffold improved local exosome retention, sustained release, and SCI repair logic.
observations.normalized_value: collagen scaffold tethers engineered exosomes for sustained delivery
observations.notes: Cargo-delivery exemplar.

observations.observation_value: bFGF-incorporated HEMA-MOETACL hydrogel implanted 5 days after T9 complete transection supported regenerating tissue ingrowth and functional recovery by 8 weeks.
observations.normalized_value: delayed bFGF hydrogel supports tissue and functional repair
observations.notes: Growth-factor hydrogel anchor.

observations.observation_value: Injectable collagen hydrogel and NT-3-loaded hydrogel increased functional recovery and altered neuronal growth, scar formation, inflammation, and collagen deposition after hemisection SCI.
observations.normalized_value: injectable NT-3 collagen scaffold supports local healing and function
observations.notes: Complements fibrin/NT-3 boundary.

observations.observation_value: NT-3/fibroin-modified gelatin sponge scaffold improved canine hindlimb movement, neural conduction, inflammation, vessel formation, synaptic structures, and nerve fiber regeneration.
observations.normalized_value: large-animal NT-3/fibroin scaffold improves conduction and repair profile
observations.notes: Large-animal cargo-release anchor.

observations.observation_value: PCL/polysialic-acid nanofiber scaffold with methylprednisolone reduced inflammatory cytokines, microglia/macrophage activation, and caspase-3 while supporting axonal regeneration and functional recovery after rat transection.
observations.normalized_value: drug-loaded nanofiber scaffold reduces inflammation and supports regeneration
observations.notes: Anti-inflammatory scaffold/cargo anchor.

observations.observation_value: PEG/PAA/protein layer-by-layer films over agarose hydrogels provided a strategy for sustained BDNF release from templated SCI channel bridges.
observations.normalized_value: agarose channel scaffold can support controlled protein release
observations.notes: Methods anchor for release rather than in vivo efficacy.

observations.evidence_type: dECM scaffold after chronic scar resection
observations.observation_value: dECM hydrogel scaffold after chronic complete SCI scar resection supported cavitary lesion filling, vascular endothelial migration, microenvironment changes, and tissue repair signals.
observations.normalized_value: chronic dECM scaffold supports histological repair after scar resection
observations.notes: Chronic ECM scaffold anchor.

observations.observation_value: dSCM/GelMA composite scaffold retained bioactive spinal cord matrix components and favorable microstructure/mechanics for stem-cell support.
observations.normalized_value: spinal-cord-derived ECM/GelMA recreates local matrix niche
observations.notes: Material microenvironment observation.

observations.observation_value: Decellularized brain matrix within HA hydrogel contributed to a composite scaffold designed to support angiogenesis, anti-inflammation, anti-apoptosis, and neural differentiation.
observations.normalized_value: brain-derived ECM is used as bioactive component in multimodal scaffold
observations.notes: ECM component cannot be isolated from other cargos.

observations.observation_value: Resected chronic human scar tissue was immunostained for CSPGs, vimentin, NF, nestin, Tuj-1, and S100 to verify scar identity before interpreting scaffold implantation.
observations.normalized_value: chronic human resection target can be scar-marker rich and neural-marker poor
observations.notes: Cross-links Module 1 lesion anatomy with scaffold targeting.

observations.evidence_type: Chronic human scar resection plus scaffold/cell
observations.observation_value: Chronic complete SCI patients underwent scar resection and NeuroRegen/hUCB-MSC implantation with safety and neurological/electrophysiological follow-up.
observations.normalized_value: chronic human scar-resection scaffold strategy feasible
observations.notes: Human chronic strategy but efficacy remains cautious.

observations.observation_value: Aligned fibrin hydrogel plus functionalized self-assembling peptide formed an interpenetrating composite presenting structural and biochemical cues to enhance SCI regeneration.
observations.normalized_value: composite hydrogel combines guidance and regulatory cues
observations.notes: Connects passive guidance to active cue delivery.

observations.outcome_type: Hydrogel and biomaterial reviews frame SCI scaffolds as bridges, delivery reservoirs, and cell carriers, but emphasize that material class, lesion geometry, and combination therapy determine interpretability.
observations.observation_value: review-map supports material- and model-specific interpretation
observations.normalized_value: N/A
observations.notes: Prevents one-size-fits-all scaffold conclusions.

observations.outcome_type: Scaffold-supported cell transplantation reviews emphasize that cells, scaffolds, growth factors, timing, and chronic lesion state interact, limiting simple causal claims.
observations.observation_value: review-map supports combination-therapy caution
observations.normalized_value: N/A
observations.notes: Supports boundary records B002 and B005.

author_claims.claim_text: Electrophysiology-guided chronic scar resection followed by NeuroRegen scaffold plus autologous BMMC implantation is feasible in a very small complete chronic human SCI cohort.
author_claims.notes: Feasibility only; efficacy remains unproven.

author_claims.claim_text: NeuroRegen scaffold plus hUCB-MSC implantation after scar resection is safe and potentially beneficial in chronic complete human SCI.
author_claims.notes: "Potentially beneficial" should be kept cautious until full clinical endpoints are extracted.

author_claims.claim_text: Porous biodegradable scaffolds can promote tissue remodeling and functional improvement in non-human primate acute SCI.
author_claims.notes: Strong translational species, abstract-level extraction.

author_claims.claim_text: Aligned hydrogel tubes provide physical guidance that can integrate with SCI lesions, reduce scarring, guide axons, support some myelination, and improve function.
author_claims.notes: Scaffold-only comparator to gelfoam makes this high-value for architecture topic.

author_claims.claim_text: Conductive aligned GelMA/CNT hydrogel fibers can combine regenerative scaffold guidance with electrical stimulation.
author_claims.notes: Needs full-text extraction for in vivo effect sizes and ES protocol.

author_claims.claim_text: Cell-seeded PLGA scaffolds can outperform scaffold-only controls after complete transection SCI.
author_claims.notes: One of the clearer scaffold-only comparator claims.

author_claims.claim_text: Decellularized peripheral nerve matrix is a clinically oriented alternative to Matrigel for Schwann-cell transplantation after SCI.
author_claims.notes: Histology benefit stronger than locomotor superiority.

author_claims.claim_text: Scaffold-delivered NT-3 can improve axonal sprouting and scar histology without necessarily improving motor function.
author_claims.notes: Important histology-function dissociation.

author_claims.claim_text: Sequential release scaffolds are designed to match SCI temporal biology by reducing early inflammation and promoting later neuronal differentiation/regeneration.
author_claims.notes: Plausible design claim; full-text timing needed.

author_claims.claim_text: Injectable ECM-derived hydrogels can integrate into lesions and support ingrowth, but ECM source and host macrophage/material response are important constraints.
author_claims.notes: Useful for avoiding "ECM is uniformly pro-regenerative" overclaim.

author_claims.claim_text: Chronic scar removal followed by anisotropic alginate hydrogel implantation can support conduction, tract growth, and locomotor recovery in chronic complete transection SCI.
author_claims.notes: Strong chronic repair signal, but abstract-level.

author_claims.claim_text: Large-animal chronic NeuroRegen scaffold plus hUC-MSC implantation after scar removal supports translational feasibility of chronic cavity/scar scaffold repair.
author_claims.notes: Needs full text for functional magnitude and scar-removal details.

author_claims.claim_text: Surface-applied scaffold patches can modulate the post-SCI inflammatory environment without requiring deep lesion-filling implantation.
author_claims.notes: Distinct intervention geometry.

author_claims.claim_text: Functional improvement after scaffold/cell treatment should not be assumed to reflect axons regenerating through the scaffold.
author_claims.notes: Directly based on negative correlation claim.

author_claims.claim_text: Acute complete human NeuroRegen scaffold plus MSC implantation is feasible to test in carefully diagnosed complete SCI, but n = 2 is not efficacy-grade evidence.
author_claims.notes: Keeps acute clinical signal appropriately constrained.

author_claims.claim_text: Collagen scaffold plus hUC-MSC repair has translational continuity across rodent, canine, and phase I human contexts.
author_claims.notes: Strong as translational path, weaker as efficacy proof.

author_claims.claim_text: Biomimetic 3D-printed spinal cord scaffolds can impose lesion-scale architecture that supports host-graft axonal relay formation.
author_claims.notes: Confounded by NPC graft but central to architecture topic.

author_claims.claim_text: Multi-channel scaffold architecture is a reusable experimental platform for separating geometry, cell, and molecule effects.
author_claims.notes: Platform claim rather than outcome claim.

author_claims.claim_text: Injectable scaffolds can be given tunable alignment after delivery, expanding guidance strategies beyond prefabricated implants.
author_claims.notes: Based on magnetic peptide hydrogel design.

author_claims.claim_text: Microchannel GelMA scaffolds can pair geometry with neural-like cell placement to improve repair after complete transection.
author_claims.notes: Cell and architecture effects not separable.

author_claims.claim_text: Chronic rat scar resection plus collagen/hUC-MSC scaffold repair improves enough outcome domains to justify chronic scaffold/cell testing.
author_claims.notes: Full-text unavailable, so claim is cautious.

author_claims.claim_text: dECM scaffold plus hNS/PC transplantation can improve chronic lesion histology even when functional recovery remains absent.
author_claims.notes: Useful histology-function boundary in chronic complete SCI.

author_claims.claim_text: Chitosan bridges with immobilized factors are best interpreted as combined scaffold-cell-cue systems rather than scaffold-only interventions.
author_claims.notes: Directly designed as combination therapy.

author_claims.claim_text: Spinal cord-derived dECM/GelMA scaffolds are used to recreate a lesion-local matrix niche for stem-cell survival and repair.
author_claims.notes: Matrix-niche claim; effect attribution remains bundled.

author_claims.claim_text: Collagen scaffold tethering can convert exosome delivery from diffuse bolus logic into a local sustained-release strategy.
author_claims.notes: Based on collagen-binding exosome design.

author_claims.claim_text: Growth-factor-loaded hydrogels can support tissue ingrowth and functional recovery after complete transection, but material and trophic effects are inseparable.
author_claims.notes: Applies to bFGF/HEMA-MOETACL system.

author_claims.claim_text: Drug-loaded nanofiber scaffolds can combine physical support with anti-inflammatory treatment after SCI.
author_claims.notes: Methylprednisolone/PCL/PSA anchor.

author_claims.claim_text: Protein-release scaffold methods matter because they can replace cell-based trophic delivery in channel-bridge strategies.
author_claims.notes: Methods anchor, not efficacy anchor.

author_claims.claim_text: HA hydrogels can be made more or less permissive by the biological identity of incorporated ECM.
author_claims.notes: Protoplasmic versus fibrous astrocyte ECM distinction.

author_claims.claim_text: Decellularized matrix components in multimodal hydrogels are usually one part of a larger regenerative cocktail.
author_claims.notes: Avoids assigning all benefit to DBM alone.

author_claims.claim_text: Chronic human scar-resection scaffold strategies depend on correctly distinguishing scar-rich target tissue from functionally important spared cord.
author_claims.notes: Cross-module alignment with Module 1B.

author_claims.claim_text: Chronic scar removal plus anisotropic scaffold channels can turn a chronic transection cavity into a guided repair substrate in rodents.
author_claims.notes: Strong chronic animal model claim.

author_claims.claim_text: Composite hydrogels can intentionally combine structural guidance with biochemical regulation, blurring scaffold and cargo categories.
author_claims.notes: AFG/fSAP anchor.

author_claims.claim_text: Surface-capping scaffolds are a distinct repair geometry from implanted lesion bridges and may be useful for modulating exposed injury environments.
author_claims.notes: HA demilune scaffold anchor.

author_claims.claim_text: The scaffold literature should be synthesized by material, cargo, cell source, injury model, chronicity, and outcome class rather than by a single global pro-repair claim.
author_claims.notes: Central synthesis caution.

author_claims.claim_text: Chronic translation requires anatomical compatibility between scaffold strategy and lesion architecture, not just a positive acute rodent repair signal.
author_claims.notes: Cross-module synthesis statement.

evidence_links.notes: Small human chronic cohort; feasibility only
Aligned tube observation supports guidance claim
Histological superiority vs Matrigel; BBB similar
3D-printed NPC scaffold also supports guidance but is cell-confounded
Supports scaffold-cell carrier idea but with different cells/material
Timed cue scaffold supports temporal-design concept
Functional dissociation also seen with NT-3 fibrin

boundary_records.boundary_statement: Human scaffold studies in Module 2A support safety/feasibility more than definitive efficacy.
Scaffold-plus-cell studies often cannot separate material effects from cell-source effects.
boundary_records.supporting_evidence: M2B-O004, O009, O011, O032-O036; review anchors M2A-R005-R007

boundary_records.boundary_statement: Histological regeneration signals do not guarantee functional recovery.
ECM-derived materials are not interchangeable; source tissue and immune response can change outcomes.
Chronic scar-resection scaffold strategies assume a lesion geometry where scar/cavity can be targeted without sacrificing spared functional tissue.
boundary_records.supporting_evidence: M2B-O001, O002, O017, O018, O046, O047; Module 1B human lesion architecture

boundary_records.boundary_statement: Functional improvement after scaffold treatment may arise from mechanisms other than axons traversing the scaffold.
Architecture/guidance observations are strongest when scaffold-only or simple controls exist; printed/cell-loaded systems often bundle geometry with graft biology.
Conductive and surface-patch scaffolds are emerging categories and should not be collapsed into passive cavity-filling scaffold claims.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Scaffold architecture can provide physical guidance for axons, cells, and tissue organization after SCI, especially when alignment, channels, or printed geometry match lesion topology.
consensus_drafts.notes: Supported by O004-O008 and O024-O029; partly confounded by cells/cargo
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Scaffold architecture can provide physical guidance for axons, cells, and tissue organization after SCI, especially when alignment, channels, or printed geometry match lesion topology.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Scaffold-supported cell transplantation improves retention and repair signals in many SCI models, but attribution is usually shared between scaffold, cell source, and cargo.
consensus_drafts.notes: Supported by O009-O011 and O030-O036; central Module 2B causality caution
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Scaffold-supported cell transplantation improves retention and repair signals in many SCI models, but attribution is usually shared between scaffold, cell source, and cargo.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Controlled release from scaffolds can improve trophic and inflammatory repair profiles, but histological benefit may not translate into motor recovery.
consensus_drafts.notes: Supported by O012-O014 and O037-O042; NT-3 fibrin gives boundary condition
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Controlled release from scaffolds can improve trophic and inflammatory repair profiles, but histological benefit may not translate into motor recovery.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: ECM and hydrogel scaffolds create permissive microenvironments, but source tissue, immune response, and lesion model determine whether integration is beneficial.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: ECM and hydrogel scaffolds create permissive microenvironments, but source tissue, immune response, and lesion model determine whether integration is beneficial.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Chronic scar/cavity scaffold repair is plausible when lesion anatomy permits resection/filling, but it must be interpreted with Module 1 lesion-boundary evidence.
consensus_drafts.notes: Supported by O001-O002, O017-O018, and O046-O047
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Chronic scar/cavity scaffold repair is plausible when lesion anatomy permits resection/filling, but it must be interpreted with Module 1 lesion-boundary evidence.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Large-animal, primate, and early human data support translational feasibility of scaffold strategies, while definitive clinical efficacy remains unestablished.
consensus_drafts.notes: Supported by O001-O003, O018, O022-O023, and O040
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Large-animal, primate, and early human data support translational feasibility of scaffold strategies, while definitive clinical efficacy remains unestablished.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Conductive, electrical, multimodal, and surface-patch scaffolds broaden scaffold repair beyond passive lesion filling by adding electrical interfaces, inflammatory modulation, or surface capping.
consensus_drafts.notes: Supported by O008, O019-O020, and O048
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Conductive, electrical, multimodal, and surface-patch scaffolds broaden scaffold repair beyond passive lesion filling by adding electrical interfaces, inflammatory modulation, or surface capping.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Module 2 scaffold evidence is best synthesized by material, cell/cargo combination, lesion chronicity, species, and outcome class rather than as a single uniformly pro-regenerative intervention family.
consensus_drafts.notes: Supported by O049-O050 and all boundary records
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Module 2 scaffold evidence is best synthesized by material, cell/cargo combination, lesion chronicity, species, and outcome class rather than as a single uniformly pro-regenerative intervention family.
